text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. During a code review, a developer’s code is checked by one or more team members before it is added to the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, when they are much easier and cheaper to fix. For example, a simple mistake in logic can be spotted immediately. Equally important is knowledge sharing. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, and the whole team becomes familiar with different parts of the codebase. Ultimately, regular code reviews improve the overall quality of the code and create a stronger, more collaborative team.
